phase3_note: 21 of 25 previously-unmapped controls are now covered by 15 new rules. 4 controls require human review and cannot be objectively measured via Azure APIs.

unmapped_controls.title: Conduct regular adversarial testing
unmapped_controls.explanation: Penetration testing, red-team exercises, and bug bounty programmes are organisational processes. Azure does not expose an API indicating whether adversarial testing has been conducted or scheduled. This control requires a human attestation process (e.g., a signed attestation in a compliance management tool, a ServiceNow record, or an external pentest report).

unmapped_controls.title: Formalize software ideation and planning management
unmapped_controls.explanation: Backlog management, sprint planning, and requirements management are implemented in Azure DevOps Boards, GitHub Projects, Jira, or similar tools — none of which surface in Azure Resource Manager. This is an organisational process control with no ARM-detectable evidence.

unmapped_controls.title: Use continuous integration
unmapped_controls.explanation: CI pipelines (Azure Pipelines, GitHub Actions, Jenkins, etc.) are not represented as ARM resources. The existence of an App Service or AKS cluster does not prove that a CI pipeline deploys to it. This control requires review of the team's DevOps toolchain configuration.

unmapped_controls.title: Optimize personnel time
unmapped_controls.explanation: Personnel time optimisation (e.g., reducing toil through automation, measuring on-call burden, right-sizing engineering teams) is a people and process concern. Azure has no API that reports on engineer hours spent on cloud operations. This is an organisational metric reviewed in retrospectives and team surveys.
